Locality: Colorado River at Imperial Dam [just N of Yuma, AZ]
Comments: Mussels found were widely scattered with no clusters of over two individuals. Estimated age of at least 6 weeks.

Locality: Pyramid Lake near the dam, approximately 250 feet deep on the intake structure to the Angeles Tunnel
Comments: One adult mussel was observed on the intake structure to the Angeles Tunnel. The observation was made via ROV video.
Two adult mussels were observed in Elderberry Forebay.  The observation was made via surface surveys.

Locality: Castaic Lake, north of Los Angeles
Comments: On August 6, 2021, a Castaic Lake park visitor reported finding one adult quagga mussel while kayaking in Castaic Lake (Los Angeles County). The specimen was confirmed as a quagga mussel by the California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) via genetic analysis. On August 17, 2021, Department of Water Resources and CDFW staff discovered a half shell (dead) quagga mussel and a full shell (dead) quagga mussel along the shoreline of Castaic Lake. On August 24, 2021, CDFW found a full shell (dead) quagga mussel attached to a rock along the shoreline.
Author 1: California Department of Water Resources
Title 1: Quagga Mussel Discovery at Castaic Lake: Boating Requirements Implemented

Locality: Castaic Lake, east side of the west arm, just north of C Point
Comments: Found approximately 10 meters above the water line.
Author 1: California Department of Water Resources
Title 1: Quagga Mussel Discovery at Castaic Lake: Boating Requirements Implemented

Locality: Angeles Tunnel, between Pyramid Lake and Elderberry Forebay
Author 1: California Department of Water Reources
Title 1: Quagga Mussel Infestation Discovered in Pyramid Lake; State Imposes Boating Restrictions

Locality: San Gabriel River, (upper) below Morris Dam
Comments: Quagga mussel veligers from the Colorado River Aqueduct were released at this location for 12 days due to an unplanned operational issue. All of the flow was fully contained within the groundwater replenishment system and no waterbodies downstream, of this engineered facility were impacted. Monitoring of the multiple downstream locations has shown no veliger detections since the initial sightings on October 26 and 31, 2017.

Locality: Castaic Creek, downstream from the Castaic Lagoon outlet
Comments: PCR and DNA sequencing performed by CDFW confirmed species to be quagga mussel (Dreissena rostriformis bugensis).

Locality: Plunge Pool below Pyramid Lake Dam
Comments: Confirmed via CPLM microscopy, PCR analysis and genetic sequencing.
Veligers detected by cross polarization microscopy.

Locality: Lake Forest Keys (Lake Forest 2) in Lake Forest
Comments: Mussels are no longer found in the waterbody due to chemical applications for algae control and are now only found within the fountain that sits in the middle of the waterbody.

Locality: Black and Gold Golf Course pond, Yorba Linda
Comments: This pond receives Colorado River water and that is the source of the infestation.

Locality: Colorado River Aqueduct at Hinds Pumping Station [near Hayfield]
Comments: This location is 125 miles downstream from the Colorado intake.

Locality: Lake Mathews [reservoir just E of Corona]
